title: Share of population who drank alcohol in the last week, United Kingdom
subtitle: Share of a given demographic who drank any alcohol in the last week.
caption: Across the United Kingdom between 2005 and 2016, the share of the population who drank alcohol in the last week varies significantly by age and sex, with older men consistently recording the highest participation rates. The line for males aged 45 to 64 remains at the top of the chart throughout the period, starting near 77% in 2005 and ending just under 70% in 2016. Males aged 65 and over and all males aged 16 and above follow closely, generally staying within the 60% to 75% range. In contrast, the lowest values appear among younger demographics; the line for females aged 16 to 24 drops from roughly 57% to below 45%, while males in the same age bracket fall from 53% to approximately 40%. Most demographic groups show a visible decline over the twelve-year span, with a notable dip occurring around 2010 for several categories. The data reveals a clear stratification where alcohol consumption frequency decreases with younger age groups and is generally higher among men than women within comparable age bands.
